name: Anat Cohen
abstract: Anat Cohen (; Hebrew: ענת כהן; born 1975) is a New York City-based jazz clarinetist, saxophonist, and bandleader from Tel Aviv, Israel.

interviews.title: Anat Cohen interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, September 9, 2021

quotes.text: No the song “Putty Boy Strut” was composed by Flying Lotus, also known as Steve Allison. But Oded composed a concerto for the tentet and myself that we premiered in Carnegie Hall a couple of years ago and it’s the title album “Triple Helix” is the name of the concerto, it’s also the title album of the last tentet album that was nominated for a Grammy for best large ensemble. Exciting.
quotes.caption: Premiering Oded's 'Triple Helix' concerto with the Tentet
quotes.doc_title: Anat Cohen interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, September 9, 2021

quotes.text: they like something, they are into it and they are dedicated to it and they’re like okay we step out of the way, even if we have worries about how you’re going to survive and what’s going to happen, we’re going to let you do what you’re want to do and support you and help you pay for music lessons and drive you around and drive you around and drive you around, and go to all your youth orchestra concerts and, you know they were there when we were squeaking and they were there when The Three Cohens, with the three of us have a sextet and Avishai my younger brother the trumpet player, and Yuval, my older brother, the saxophone player, we have a sextet and when The Three Cohens Sextet played Carnegie Hall my mother was sitting in the audience and you know, and we introduced her and she got up and she was like doing the royal wave to everybody. So that’s the payment they get.
quotes.caption: The Three Cohens Sextet's Carnegie Hall debut with mother in audience
quotes.doc_title: Anat Cohen interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, September 9, 2021

about_quotes.speaker: George Wein
about_quotes.evidence: I call Anat, 'Would you like to play with me?' 'It would be an honor.' ... And that was the band I had. ... We're all friends and we swung the whole week.
about_quotes.doc_title: George Wein — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Schuller Gunther
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] ...I don't know if you know of her work. [INTERVIEWEE] No.
about_quotes.doc_title: Schuller Gunther — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Karolina Strassmayer
about_quotes.evidence: It’s what my friend Anat Cohen used to call “Neanderthal interaction.”
about_quotes.doc_title: Karolina Strassmayer interviewed by Monk Rowe, May 25, 2023

about_quotes.speaker: Stanley Kay
about_quotes.evidence: Anat Cohen is mentioned as a member of the interviewee's band ('Sherrie and my band') and as an idol for young musicians, but no direct personal interaction or specific collaborative history between Stanley Kay and Anat Cohen is described in the text.
about_quotes.doc_title: Stanley Kay interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clearwater Beach, Florida, March 18, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Hentoff Nat
about_quotes.evidence: Then, in New York, the name who comes to mind happens to be an Israeli, who’s been playing here for a long time. That’s Anat Cohen. She fits into any kind of first-class jazz situation.
about_quotes.doc_title: Hentoff Nat — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Dan Morgenstern
about_quotes.evidence: I was very happy when Anat Cohen started winning the clarinet thing in the DownBeat Critics Poll... And Anat, can do that too. She plays with Ostwald too when she’s in town.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dan Morgenstern interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, April 9, 2013
